Overview
VITEM II applies to foreigners travelling to Brazil for the purpose of receiving health treatment. It may also be granted, at the discretion of the Consular Authority, to a person accompanying the foreigner travelling to Brazil for medical treatment.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
VITEM II applies to foreigners travelling to Brazil for the purpose of receiving health treatment. It may also be granted, at the discretion of the Consular Authority, to a person accompanying the foreigner travelling to Brazil for medical treatment.

## Visa Fees
Visitor Visa

[Tourism]
Temporary Visa

[Tourism 2]
FEES FOR US CITIZENS

[Tourism 3]
FEES FOR UK PASSPORT HOLDER

[Tourism 4]
Application service fee
The service fee, mandatory for all visa applications, is 27.8 CNY per application inclusive of taxes. This fee corresponds to the standard application processing service provided by VFS.

## Documents Required
Please click here to check the documents required.

## Photo Specifications
Please submit one passport photo which should fulfil the following criteria (conforming to ICAO standards).
- Colour photo on a white background
- Size: 4 x 5cm
- Taken from the front with the subject looking directly at the camera
- The mouth must be closed, no teeth visible
- Without any shadow on the face
- Wearer of glasses: the eyes must not be covered by the frames of the glasses. The glasses cannot be coloured and there’s no reflection on the glasses.
- Face covers 70-80% of the photo
- Recent photo, not older than 6 months
- Photo should not be modified with Photoshop
- be printed on high quality photo-paper with high resolution
- Religious head-dresses are allowed provided that all details of the face are clearly shown. Chin, forehead and both cheeks shall all be clearly visible and shadows in the face due to the head-dress are not allowed

## Processing Time

[Tourism 5]
- Under certain circumstances (e.g. excessive number of applications, technical malfunction, etc) visas might take longer to be processed.

## Download Forms
Online Visa Application Form (https://formulario-mre.serpro.gov.br/sci/pages/web/ui/#/instrucoes-iniciais-visto). The application form should be filled–out online and the Visa Application Receipt (RER) should be printed out and signed. The signature on the Visa Application Form Receipt – RER receipt must match the applicant’s signature on his/her passport.
According to the Personal Information Protection Law of the People’s Republic of China and other applicable laws and regulations to protect your personal information, VFS Global must obtain the consent from you for the processing of your personal data and proceed with your visa application. Please download, print, fill, sign and submit the consent form along with your visa application form, without which we will not be able to proceed with the application lodgment. Click here

## Overview
This type of visa is required for athletes, artists, fashion models or entertainers, as well as their supporting technical staff, who wish to perform in Brazil.
This visa request is submitted to the Ministry of Justice and Public Security in Brazil by the sponsoring institution or company in Brazil. Once the request is approved, the Ministry of External Relations will send the approval to the Embassy or Consulate.
Qualified applicants include:
- Professional artists;
- Musicians;
- Sportspeople;
- Fashion models;
- All accompanying technicians/assistants.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa (VIVIS)
visa_types.overview:
Visitor Visa (VIVIS)

The Visit Visa (VIVIS) may be granted to a visitor entering the Federative Republic of Brazil for a stay not exceeding ninety (90) days, provided that the visitor does not intend to establish residence and will not engage in remunerated activities in the national territory, except for payments made as per diem, allowance, fee, pro labore, or other reimbursements related to travel expenses.
visa_types.full_text:
## Visitor Visa (VIVIS)

The Visit Visa (VIVIS) may be granted to a visitor entering the Federative Republic of Brazil for a stay not exceeding ninety (90) days, provided that the visitor does not intend to establish residence and will not engage in remunerated activities in the national territory, except for payments made as per diem, allowance, fee, pro labore, or other reimbursements related to travel expenses.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

- for a foreign national holding a protocol of application for registration, extension of stay, or residence authorization in Brazil;
- for a national of another country aged sixty (60) years or older, or a person with special needs, holder of a permanent residence permit, whose National Migration Registration Card (CRNM) has expired;
Brazilian legislation requires all foreign residents to keep their migratory status up to date and to renew their registration within the legally established deadlines. Holders of a permanent residence permit whose National Migration Registration Card (CRNM) has expired are, therefore, expected to apply for a new residence visa (VITEM) to regularize their situation. Exceptionally, the law allows foreign nationals aged sixty (60) years or older, or persons with disabilities (PCD), who hold a permanent residence permit and whose CRNM has expired, to apply for a visitor visa (VIVIS) to return to Brazil, provided they have not remained outside Brazilian territory for more than two consecutive years.
It is also possible for foreign nationals holding a valid RNE or CRNM whose document was lost, stolen, destroyed, or misplaced to request a visitor's visa (VIVIS) for the purpose of regularizing their situation without having to reapply for a residence visa. In addition, a visitor visa may be issued to foreign nationals who hold a protocol of application for registration, extension of stay, or residence authorization in Brazil, upon presentation of the protocol issued by the competent authority.
In both cases, the visitor visa may be issued only to those who in fact reside in Brazil and intend to return to continue residing there. The visa will be issued for single entry and valid for 90 days.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
VITEM I should be requested by researchers, scientists and visiting professors – with no employment relationship with a Brazilian institution – for the purposes of academic research, scientific cooperation, academic extension programs, and teaching, as long as the stay is set to last longer than 90 days. If the stay does not exceed 90 days, only a Visitor Visa (VIVIS) is needed.
For researchers, scientists and visiting professors – with employment relationship with a Brazilian institution – the process must be initiated in Brazil, by the sponsoring institution (on behalf of the applicant), with submission of the pertinent application and supporting documentation to the Ministry of Justice and Public Security. Once the procedure in Brazil is concluded and approved, the authorization will be transmitted to the Embassy/Consulate via the Brazilian Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Only then will the applicant be able to proceed and lodge the visa application itself.
Please note: a VITEM I does not apply to study / research exchange purposes (except postdoctoral degree studies, which does require VITEM I). For activities involving academic qualification such as participation in "sandwich" and university collaboration programs, carrying out part of one’s master's / doctor's degree research, doing one's compulsory undergraduate / postgraduate study module abroad (commonly referred to as "year abroad"), one must obtain a Student Visa (VITEM IV) instead.
Researchers:
VITEM I for the purpose of research may be granted to a researcher or scientist fulfilling the following conditions:
- I - when receiving a scholarship granted by foundations supporting public or private higher education and scientific and technological research institutions, for conducting research at a Brazilian public or private higher education institution recognized by the Ministry of Education or by other national technical and scientific institutions recognized by the Ministry of Science and Technology
- II - when receiving a scholarship funded by the National Council for Scientific and Technological Development (CNPq), the Coordination for the Improvement of Higher Education Personnel (CAPES), the Financier of Studies and Projects (FINEP), and other public research support foundations;
- III – when travelling as a scientist, researcher, or professional who comes to Brazil under an international agreement, as recognized by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs;
- IV – when travelling to participate in research projects established between foreign institutions or research and development centers of foreign companies with:
- a national company;
- a national company incubator;
- Scientific, Technological, and Innovation Institution (ICT);
- Technological Innovation Center (NIT);
- support foundation;
- technology park;
- technology hub; and
- public and private educational and research institutions.
- V – when conducting, as a scientist, researcher, or professional, research not regulated by Decree No. 98,830 of 1990;
- VI – when participating of a postdoctoral program.
- If the applicant will not be receiving any honorarium for his/her presentation, and stay no more than 90 days, he/she will be eligible for a VIVIS VII (Study, internship, research or scientific cooperation).
Teaching and academic extension
VITEM I for the purposes of teaching and academic extension may be granted to foreigners under the following conditions:
- I - professor, without an employment relationship with a Brazilian institution, when supported by an institution in their country of origin or origin, under an inter-institutional agreement or similar instruments between the interested Brazilian higher education institution and the foreign higher education or research institution; and
- II - a recipient of a scholarship granted by a Brazilian higher education institution, public or private, recognized by the Ministry of Education;
VITEM I for the purposes of teaching and academic extension with an employment relationship with a Brazilian institution require an authorization from the Ministry of Justice and Public Security. Only then will the Embassy / Consulate be authorized to issue the visa.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Business encompasses participation in meetings, trade fairs, and business events; prospecting of commercial opportunities; signing of contracts; and performance of audit or consultancy activities, as well as provision of technical assistance services or transfer of technology under a contract, cooperation agreement, or arrangement between a foreign and a Brazilian legal entity provided that the visa holder is not remunerated by any Brazilian entity and that the activity performed does not exceed a period of ninety (90) days. The visa may be granted if the foreign national receives payment from the government, a Brazilian employer, or a private entity in the form of per diem, allowance, fee, pro labore, or other reimbursements related to travel expenses.
Following the publication of Decree No. 12,657 of October 7, 2025, please note the following change in visa procedures:
The decree updates Article 29 of Decree No. 9,199, expanding the situations in which a Visitor Visa (VIVIS) may be granted. It now allows the issuance of a VIVIS for the provision of technical assistance services or transfer of technology under a contract, cooperation agreement, or arrangement between a foreign and a Brazilian legal entity, provided that the maximum intended stay does not exceed 90 days.
This change does not modify Normative Resolution No. 03/2017. Therefore, for stays that do not exceed 90 days, the sponsoring company may choose either to apply for a prior residence authorization for the purpose of obtaining a Temporary Visa (VITEM V), or alternatively, to apply directly for a Visitor Visa (VIVIS) at the Visa Center.
For stays exceeding 90 days, the application for a Temporary Visa remains mandatory.

## Overview
This type of visa requires starting the process in Brazil. The Brazilian company or institution must file a petition for temporary residency authorization at the Ministry of Justice and only after the authorization is granted, the applicant will be able to submit the application.
Applicants eligible for VITEM IX (Investment) include:
- Administrators, managers, directors, or executives with supervising responsibilities over a foreign corporation or society that intends to invest in a Brazilian counterpart;
- Individuals who intend to invest at least R$ 500.000,00 in a Brazilian company/enterprise;
- Individuals who intend to invest at least R$ 150.000,00 in a Brazilian startup;
- Individuals who intend to invest at least R$ 700.000,00 in real estate in the North or Northeast regions of Brazil;

## Overview
Note: Please be advised that all VITEM V requests must be submitted to the Coordination General of Labor Immigration of the Ministry of Justice and Public Security in Brazil by the sponsoring institution before the application is submitted to the Embassy or Consulate.
Once the request is approved by the Coordination General of Labor Immigration of the Ministry and Justice and Public Security in Brazil, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs will communicate said approval to the specified Consulate which may issue the visa.
For further information, refer to: < https://portaldeimigracao.mj.gov.br/pt/ (https://portaldeimigracao.mj.gov.br/pt/) >
The temporary visa for work in Brazil includes persons hired by a Brazilian company or individual, technical jobs and/or training performed at an affiliate company in Brazil, franchise operation activities, evaluation and/or repair of equipment installed in Brazil or sold to a Brazilian client, etc. Whether or not you will be compensated by a Brazilian party is not necessarily a decisive factor. Please be advised that Temporary visas may be granted to applicants who intend to establish temporary residence (for more than 90 days). If you intend to stay in Brazil for less than 90 days, you must apply for a Visit Visa.
Qualified applicants include:
- Professionals traveling to provide technical assistance or transfer technology/knowledge within a period of 90 days;
- Scientists, professors, teachers, journalists, technicians, or any other traveler holding an employment contract with a Brazilian employer;
- Professionals traveling to render service to the Brazilian Government;
- Students in professional training, working without a contract, after completion of a technical school or university course;
- Medical residents or interns;
- Language teachers, including trainees; and
- Ship crewmembers working on foreign fishing vessels rented by Brazilian companies

Overview
VITEM VII (Religious Activity) applies to clergymen and missionaries with theological or academic religious education who intend to go to Brazil for religious purposes, without an employment relationship in Brazil, at the invitation of a corresponding religious institution in Brazil. The religious nature of the interested party's mission is essential for the granting of VITEM
VII. Even if the foreigner holds a title from a religious or denominational order of any nature, if the purpose of their trip is not to perform a religious mission in Brazil, VITEM VII is not applicable.
Please be advised that Temporary visas may be granted to applicants who intend to establish temporary residence (for more than 90 days). If you intend to stay in Brazil for less than 90 days, you must apply for a Visit Visa.

Overview
Typical applicants are students participating in exchange programs, internships or short-term courses in Brazil, for example students pursuing: (i) technical short-term studies (such as Portuguese and Brazilian culture classes); (ii) undergraduate, graduate or postgraduate academic or theological studies; (iii) internships; (iv) students on scholarship or exchange programs with a Brazilian institution of higher education.
Please note:
- Students and exchange visitors must be accepted by their schools or program sponsors in Brazil before applying for visas. The school must be accredited by the Ministry of Education of Brazil (MEC).
- Visa holders are prohibited from holding any paid job in Brazil.
- Please be reminded that a apostilled birth certificate translated in Portuguese should be presented while registering at the Federal Police Station in Brazil

## Overview
Typical applicants are students participating in exchange programs, internships or short-term courses in Brazil, for example students pursuing: (i) technical short-term studies (such as Portuguese and Brazilian culture classes); (ii) undergraduate, graduate or postgraduate academic or theological studies; (iii) internships; (iv) students on scholarship or exchange programs with a Brazilian institution of higher education.
Please note:

## Documents Required
Please click here to check the documents required.

## Photo Specifications
Please submit one passport photo which should fulfil the following criteria (conforming to ICAO standards).
- Colour photo on a white background
- Size: 4 x 5cm
- Taken from the front with the subject looking directly at the camera
- The mouth must be closed, no teeth visible
- Without any shadow on the face
- Wearer of glasses: the eyes must not be covered by the frames of the glasses. The glasses cannot be coloured and there’s no reflection on the glasses.
- Face covers 70-80% of the photo
- Recent photo, not older than 6 months
- Photo should not be modified with Photoshop

- Religious head-dresses are allowed provided that all details of the face are clearly shown. Chin, forehead and both cheeks shall all be clearly visible and shadows in the face due to the head-dress are not allowed

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Family reunification visa allows family members of Brazilian nationals or family members of foreign nationals to reside in Brazil.
Applicants eligible for a Family Reunification Visa include:
- Spouse or partner in accordance with Brazilian law.
- Child of a Brazilian or an immigrant with a residence permit.
- Stepchild of a Brazilian or an immigrant with a residence permit provided that they are under eighteen, until the age of twenty-four if they are a student, or of any age if they are dependent on the sponsor.
- Parent of an immigrant with a residence permit.
- Ascendant up to the second degree of a Brazilian or an immigrant with a residence permit.
- Descendant up to the second degree of a Brazilian or an immigrant with a residence permit.
- Sibling of a Brazilian or an immigrant with a residence permit, provided that they are under the age of eighteen, or up to the age of twenty-four if they are a student, or of any age if they are dependent on the sponsor.
- Guardian, curator, or legal guardian of a Brazilian citizen.
Notes:
Parent of a Brazilian citizen: the visa can only be issued if the parent will reside with the child at the same address in Brazil.
A Family Reunification Visa cannot be granted if the sponsor is in Brazil on a Family Reunification Visa or if the marriage was performed by proxy.
